Sustainable Irrigation Is Nexus Thinking In Action - global-agriculture.com They were explored in a panel of 10 experts at the 2026 Global Forum for Food and Agriculture, titled Sustainable Irrigation Requires a Water-Energy-Food-Environment (WEFE) Nexus Approach. The panel took on this challenge, using the nexus of small-scale investments in groundwater-fed irrigation to demonstrate how WEFE nexus thinking becomes meaningful when it is translated into action oriented tools, programs, and governance choices, with illustrations from Haiti, Mali and Pakistan. At the government’s request, the WEFE Nexus Policy Area of Work under the CGIAR Policy Innovations Program has developed web-based Solar Suitability Mapping and Solar Irrigation Pump Sizing tools to support the investment decision for installing new solar pumps as well as to better balance water and farming needs. The irrigation technologies are simple, but from the very beginning – before the WEFE nexus concept was elaborated – Welthungerhilfe considered the irrigation systems as part of a food security system, taking into account how water is managed, how ecosystems are protected, and how local institutions function. https://www.global-agriculture.com/ag-tech-research-news/sustainable-irrigation-is-nexus-thinking-in-action/

Electric Agriculture Machines & Fencing: 2026 Innovations - Farmonaut **Key Insight:** High-efficiency **IE3 and IE4 agriculture electric motors**, coupled with **VFDs** and smart starters, can slash energy use by up to 30% compared to legacy systems—making electrification a highly economic and sustainable move for every farm. The momentum in **electric agriculture machines**—from **harvesters and tedders to sprayers, balers, greenhouse systems and climate control fans**—signals a transformative leap in how field and protected environments are managed. **Electric agriculture machines** offer **higher efficiency, lower emissions, less maintenance, quieter operation, and better integration with smart farm management tools.** They help reduce operational costs, environmental impact, and ensure greater safety for operators and livestock. \*\*Electric motors, pumps, and fence systems\*\* can be directly paired with IoT sensors, AI-driven controllers, and wireless management software to automate irrigation, feeding, security, and climate control—boosting productivity and reliability. Whether you manage a small family plot or a multi-thousand-hectare agribusiness, **now is the time to invest in electric agriculture machines, smart fencing, and robust energy systems**. https://farmonaut.com/blogs/electric-agriculture-machines-fencing-2026-innovations

Improving Women’s Livelihoods Through Water-smart Post-harvest Innovation - global-agriculture.com # Improving Women’s Livelihoods Through Water-smart Post-harvest Innovation. This World Water Day 2026, an ICARDA-led initiative funded by the Government of Japan, shows how rural women in Egypt are improving water productivity through post-harvest innovation. These units, equipped with co-designed hybrid solar dryers, vegetable slicers, grinders, and sesame oil extractors, enable women to process surplus produce, reduce post-harvest losses, and extend the shelf life of perishable crops. For tomatoes, drying reduced post-harvest losses from 30% to 10%, increasing water productivity by 28%. As women process and package their products, they are not just improving their livelihoods, but also strengthening the resilience of entire food systems in the face of rising prices and climate pressures. Women-led post-harvest initiatives in Egypt’s dryland farming systems hold significant potential to increase water productivity and strengthen food security. Through ongoing innovation and partnerships with cooperatives, NGOs, and the Egyptian Ministry of Water Resources and Irrigation, ICARDA is supporting women in building a more resilient and sustainable agricultural future. https://www.global-agriculture.com/ag-tech-research-news/improving-womens-livelihoods-through-water-smart-post-harvest-innovation/
